title: Retrieve then summarize predictive analytics
mission: Step 1: Use the corpus to find what techniques predictive analytics combines. Step 2: Summarize your retrieved evidence into at most 4 bullet points in English. Step 3: Finish with filenames you relied on.

title: Two-step compare BI and business analytics
mission: First retrieve passages about business intelligence. Then retrieve passages about business analytics. Finally explain in 4-6 sentences how the articles contrast BI with business analytics, citing only retrieved filenames.

title: Big data characteristics
mission: Retrieve evidence about volume/velocity/variety (or related definitions) for big data. Summarize the top retrieved passage if it is long. Provide a short final answer suitable for an MSBA slide.

title: Machine learning paradigms
mission: Find corpus passages about supervised vs unsupervised learning. Summarize differences in bullets, then give a one-paragraph conclusion with sources.

title: Operations research example
mission: Retrieve information about a classic operations research problem (e.g., resource allocation or optimization). Summarize the story in 3 bullets and finish.

title: Data science and statistics
mission: Locate what the data science article says about statistics. If the combined retrieved text is long, summarize it before writing the final answer.

title: Business analytics modeling
mission: Retrieve how business analytics uses modeling and statistics. Produce a concise final answer with sources; use summarize_text only if needed.

title: Relevance of embeddings (implicit)
mission: Using only the corpus, explain what dense embeddings are used for in retrieval-style workflows described or implied across articles. Retrieve as needed, then finish.

title: Iterative refine
mission: First retrieve broadly about 'customer analytics'. If snippets are noisy, run a second narrower retrieve. Then finish with the best-supported statement.

title: Executive TL;DR
mission: Retrieve key points about business intelligence tooling history. Summarize the retrieved material into an executive TL;DR of max 5 bullets, then finish with sources.
